intro: Bolelia, also known as Bolella or Boletin, is a genus of flowering plants in the family Solanaceae, consisting of a few species of shrubs or small trees native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Bolelia include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Bolelia is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, or made into a syrup or tincture, to treat various respiratory conditions and other ailments.
